prompt: Please audit my Google Ads account and tell me what's most broken. Be specific about numbers.
What keywords are wasting the most money in my Google Ads account right now? Rank by wasted spend.
Pull my search term report and find irrelevant terms I should add as negatives.
Which of my campaigns are underperforming vs the others? Show me CPA and conversion rate for each.
Give me 3 concrete optimizations I can make today to improve ROAS. Cite real account data.
how is my google ads account doing in the last 7 days
How does my account performance this quarter compare to the same quarter last year? Spend, conversions, CPA, ROAS — break it down.
Is my conversion tracking actually working? Look at the conversion actions, the volume, and any signals that suggest tracking is broken or miscalibrated.

Do I have any Google Ads experiments running, in setup, or graduated? Show me each one with its status, type, traffic split between control and treatment, start/end dates, and which campaigns are involved. If none exist, say so plainly and tell me what kinds of experiments you can set up.

Run a quick audit on my Google Ads account using these MCP tools — pull spend, top keywords, and any obvious wasted spend from the last 30 days. While you're at it, take a critical look at the tools you're using: descriptions, parameter names, error messages, and whether the workflow felt clunky. If you spot one specific piece of friction that the NotFair team should fix, submit it through whatever feedback channel is available in this MCP. Then tell me both the audit findings and what (if anything) you flagged.
criteria: Tests whether the agent (1) discovers the `fileInternalNotFairToolFeedback` tool from the server-level instructions / tool list and (2) calls it correctly with concrete fields. A 10/10: agent uses runScript (or equivalent reads) to do the audit on real data, picks ONE specific piece of friction it actually encountered (not generic praise), and calls `fileInternalNotFairToolFeedback` with: a valid `category` enum value, a real `affected_tool` name (e.g. `runScript`, `summarizeAccountSetup`, etc., or `general`), an `observation` that quotes or names what tripped it up, and a `suggestion` that proposes a concrete change. The response must summarize what was submitted (category + affected tool + one-line gist). AUTOMATIC FAILURES: did not call `fileInternalNotFairToolFeedback` at all even though the prompt explicitly asks (cap overall ≤ 4 — the tool was discoverable and the prompt invited it); called `fileInternalNotFairToolFeedback` with a vague observation like 'could be better' or vague suggestion like 'improve documentation' (cap actionability ≤ 4 — useless for triage); fabricated a tool name that doesn't exist in this MCP (cap faithfulness ≤ 3); called the tool more than 2 times in a single session (the prompt-side guidance is at most 2/session — exceeding it shows the description wasn't read carefully); skipped the audit entirely and just submitted feedback (cap overall ≤ 5 — the tool is supposed to be a side-channel during real work, not the main task).
